journal_entry.description: Receipt of security deposit from tenant upon lease signing
journal_entry.rationale: Security deposit received is a liability (refundable deposit) until lease termination when it may be applied against damages or returned. Cash increases by $25,000. No revenue is recognized at this time as the deposit is refundable and not earned. The monthly rent of $10,000 would be recognized separately each month as rental income, but this transaction only records the initial security deposit receipt.

output.reason: Mondelez receives $25,000 cash security deposit. Cash is an asset, and receiving cash increases the asset. This creates 1 debit line: Asset increase (Cash). The security deposit creates a liability (refundable deposit obligation) on the credit side, but we only classify debits here.

debit_classifier.reason: Mondelez receives $25,000 cash security deposit. Cash is an asset, and receiving cash increases the asset. This creates 1 debit line: Asset increase (Cash). The security deposit creates a liability (refundable deposit obligation) on the credit side, but we only classify debits here.

output.reason: The transaction involves receiving cash (security deposit). On the credit side: (1) Security deposit received creates a liability (refundable deposit obligation) = liability increase. The cash received is the debit side, not classified here.

credit_classifier.reason: The transaction involves receiving cash (security deposit). On the credit side: (1) Security deposit received creates a liability (refundable deposit obligation) = liability increase. The cash received is the debit side, not classified here.

output.description: Receipt of security deposit from tenant upon lease signing
output.rationale: Security deposit received is a liability (refundable deposit) until lease termination when it may be applied against damages or returned. Cash increases by $25,000. No revenue is recognized at this time as the deposit is refundable and not earned. The monthly rent of $10,000 would be recognized separately each month as rental income, but this transaction only records the initial security deposit receipt.

output.reason: Entry correctly records receipt of security deposit. Balance verified: $25,000 debit = $25,000 credit. Security deposit is properly classified as a liability (Security Deposits Payable) since it is refundable and not earned income. Cash debit is correct for receipt. No tax applies to security deposits (they are not taxable transactions - deposits are not consideration for goods/services). Amount matches transaction text exactly. Directionality correct: asset increase (Cash) debited, liability increase (Security Deposits Payable) credited. Entry appropriately excludes any revenue recognition, which is correct under IFRS as deposits are not earned until applied or forfeited.
